talks.title: The Power of Vulnerability
talks.speaker: Amira Hassan
talks.speakerBio: Amira Hassan is a clinical psychologist and mental health advocate with over 8 years of experience in helping individuals overcome emotional barriers. She specializes in trauma recovery and has worked with diverse populations across the Middle East, combining evidence-based therapeutic practices with culturally-sensitive approaches.

talks.title: Rethinking Education in the AI Era
talks.speaker: Omar El-Sayed
talks.speakerBio: Omar El-Sayed is an EdTech innovator and founder of an AI-powered learning platform used by over 50,000 students across the MENA region. With a background in computer science and education, he focuses on bridging the gap between technology and personalized learning, believing that AI should enhance rather than replace human educators.

talks.title: Design Thinking for Social Impact
talks.speaker: Layla Mansour
talks.speakerBio: Layla Mansour is a social design strategist who has led design-thinking workshops for NGOs, government agencies, and social enterprises throughout Egypt. Her work focuses on creating human-centered solutions to pressing social challenges, combining design principles with community engagement and sustainable development practices.

talks.title: Breaking the Silence on Mental Health
talks.speaker: Karim Nabil
talks.speakerBio: Karim Nabil is a psychiatrist and mental health advocate dedicated to breaking stigma around mental illness in the Arab world. Through his nonprofit organization, he provides free mental health services and awareness campaigns, advocating for policy changes that prioritize mental wellbeing as a fundamental human right.

talks.title: Sustainable Cities: A Blueprint
talks.speaker: Nour Abdelrahman
talks.speakerBio: Nour Abdelrahman is an urban planner and environmental scientist working on sustainable city development projects across the Middle East. She specializes in creating green infrastructure, promoting renewable energy adoption, and designing communities that balance economic growth with environmental conservation and quality of life.

talks.title: The Art of Storytelling
talks.speaker: Yasmin Farouk
talks.speakerBio: Yasmin Farouk is a writer, filmmaker, and cultural producer known for her compelling narratives about contemporary Arab identity. Through her multimedia projects, she explores themes of heritage, belonging, and transformation, working to preserve and celebrate the rich stories of her community while making them accessible to global audiences.

talks.title: Entrepreneurship in Emerging Markets
talks.speaker: Ahmed Soliman
talks.speakerBio: Ahmed Soliman is a serial entrepreneur and business advisor who has founded and scaled three successful startups in the MENA region. He is passionate about supporting young entrepreneurs in emerging markets, providing mentorship and investment through his venture fund that focuses on sustainable and inclusive business models creating real social impact.

talks.title: Music as Medicine
talks.speaker: Salma Khaled
talks.speakerBio: Salma Khaled is a music therapist and composer who has pioneered music therapy programs in hospitals and rehabilitation centers throughout Egypt. Her research demonstrates the profound healing power of music in treating anxiety, depression, and chronic pain, merging traditional Arabic music with contemporary therapeutic techniques.

2025.title: Chairwoman
2025.name: Sama El-Badrawy
2025.description: The Chairman oversees the entire TEDxGUC operation, ensuring the event aligns with TED's mission of spreading ideas worth sharing.
2025.committeeDescription: The Chairman's office sets the strategic direction for the entire TEDxGUC organization. Responsible for external partnerships, TED licensing compliance, and ensuring every division works in harmony toward delivering a world-class event.
2025.memberBio: Sama is a business administration graduate and visionary leader passionate about creating platforms for transformative ideas. With experience in event management and community building, she brings strategic thinking and collaborative spirit to the role.

children.title: Curator — Talks
children.name: Yehia Amr
children.description: Leads the Talks division, curating speakers and ensuring content quality aligns with the event's theme.
children.committeeDescription: The Talks division is the heart of TEDxGUC. This team scouts, selects, and develops speakers — ensuring every talk is compelling, well-researched, and delivered with impact.
children.memberBio: Yehia is an engineering student with a deep passion for innovative storytelling and public discourse. He combines his analytical mindset with creative vision to identify speakers who challenge conventions and inspire audiences.

children.title: Coaching
children.name: Mariam Adel
children.description: Coaches speakers through rehearsals and presentation skills workshops.
children.committeeDescription: The Coaching committee works one-on-one with speakers to refine their narratives, improve delivery, and ensure every talk resonates with the audience.
children.memberBio: Mariam is a psychology student with experience in public speaking coaching and debate.

children.title: Research & Production
children.name: Hassan Tarek
children.description: Conducts research to support speakers and manages the production pipeline.
children.committeeDescription: Research & Production ensures every talk is backed by solid research and produced to the highest standards — from script development to stage design.
children.memberBio: Hassan is a political science student with a keen eye for detail and a love for investigative research.

children.title: Curator — Operations
children.name: Kareem Shawish
children.description: Manages all operational logistics, event planning, and day-of execution.
children.committeeDescription: Operations is the backbone of TEDxGUC. From venue logistics to vendor management, this division ensures every detail is accounted for so the event runs flawlessly.
children.memberBio: Kareem is a systems engineer known for his meticulous attention to detail and problem-solving prowess. He thrives under pressure and excels at orchestrating complex operations, ensuring every element aligns perfectly for a seamless event experience.

children.title: Events Management

children.description: Plans and executes all event activities.
children.committeeDescription: Events Management handles venue coordination, scheduling, and on-site attendee experience from doors-open to closing remarks.
children.memberBio: Dina is an architecture student who brings her spatial awareness and creativity to event design.

children.title: Partnerships
children.name: Ali Mahmoud
children.description: Builds relationships with sponsors and stakeholders.
children.committeeDescription: Partnerships secures sponsorships and strategic alliances that fund and elevate the TEDxGUC experience.
children.memberBio: Ali is a finance student with strong networking skills and a talent for negotiation.

children.title: Logistics
children.name: Rana Hossam
children.description: Handles transportation, equipment, catering, and venue setup.
children.committeeDescription: Logistics manages the physical infrastructure of the event — from equipment rental and catering to transportation and setup.
children.memberBio: Rana is an industrial engineering student who thrives on solving complex logistical puzzles.

children.title: Curator — OD
children.name: Hazem Talhawy
children.description: Focuses on team growth, internal culture, and organizational excellence.
children.committeeDescription: Organizational Development nurtures the TEDxGUC team itself — running training programs, building culture, and ensuring every member grows alongside the organization.
children.memberBio: Hazem is an organizational psychology specialist dedicated to building high-performing teams and fostering inclusive cultures. He believes that a thriving organization starts with investing in people and creating spaces where everyone can contribute their best.

children.title: Associate Development
children.name: Youssef Gamal
children.description: Develops training programs for new associates.
children.committeeDescription: Associate Development creates onboarding programs, workshops, and mentorship tracks for new team members.
children.memberBio: Youssef is an HR management student who believes in investing in people as the key to organizational success.

children.title: Associate Experience
children.name: Farida Nasser

children.committeeDescription: Associate Experience designs team-bonding activities, feedback systems, and wellness initiatives to keep morale high.
children.memberBio: Farida is a psychology student dedicated to creating inclusive and supportive team environments.

children.title: Curator — Marketing
children.name: Jana Nader
children.description: Directs all marketing, branding, and communication strategies.
children.committeeDescription: Marketing & Coordination amplifies the TEDxGUC brand through social media, content creation, and integrated campaigns that drive engagement and ticket sales.
children.memberBio: Jana is a creative marketing strategist with a flair for storytelling and data-driven decision making. She combines bold creative vision with analytical rigor to craft campaigns that resonate with diverse audiences and drive meaningful engagement.

children.memberBio: Ammar is a communication coach and performance strategist who has worked with hundreds of speakers across diverse fields. He excels at identifying a speaker's authentic voice and helping them communicate their ideas with clarity, passion, and impact.

children.title: Research & Production
children.name: Abdulrahman Waleed
children.description: Researching cutting-edge topics and managing production.
children.committeeDescription: Research & Production ensures every talk is backed by credible sources and produced with professional-grade quality.
children.memberBio: Abdulrahman is a meticulous researcher and producer who brings talks to life through rigorous research and creative production direction. His commitment to authenticity and quality ensures that every idea presented on stage is both intellectually sound and emotionally compelling.

children.memberBio: Hamza is a relationship builder with strong business acumen and diplomatic skills. He understands that great partnerships are built on mutual value and trust, and he works diligently to create win-win collaborations that elevate TEDxGUC and benefit all stakeholders.

children.memberBio: Abdallah is a logistics coordinator with a natural talent for solving complex supply chain puzzles. His meticulous planning and vendor management ensure that all physical elements of the event are perfectly coordinated to create a seamless attendee experience.

children.title: Curator — OD

children.title: Associate Development
children.name: Rohanda Ahmed

children.memberBio: Rohanda is a talent development specialist who sees potential in every team member and creates pathways for growth and excellence. Her mentorship programs are known for transforming first-time volunteers into confident leaders.

children.title: Associate Experience
children.name: Abdullah Abosamra

children.committeeDescription: Associate Experience ensures every team member feels valued through social events, recognition programs, and well-being support.
children.memberBio: Abdullah is a well-being advocate and event creator who designs meaningful experiences that strengthen team bonds and celebrate individual contributions. He believes that a happy team is a productive team.

children.title: Curator — Marketing
children.name: Laila Eid

children.committeeDescription: Marketing & Coordination drives brand awareness, manages social channels, and creates integrated campaigns to engage the TEDxGUC community.
children.memberBio: Laila is a forward-thinking marketing leader with expertise in digital strategy and community engagement. She crafts compelling brand narratives that resonate across platforms and creates campaigns that spark conversation and drive meaningful audience connection.

children.title: Media & Design
children.name: Farida Sabrah
children.description: Producing visual storytelling content across all platforms.
children.committeeDescription: Media & Design creates the visual identity of TEDxGUC — social media graphics, event branding, video production, and stage visuals.
children.memberBio: Farida is a multimedia artist and designer with a gift for translating complex ideas into stunning visual narratives. Her creative work consistently elevates the TEDxGUC brand and captivates audiences across all touchpoints.

children.title: Campaigns & Copy
children.name: Malak Hatem
children.description: Crafting compelling narratives and managing multi-channel campaigns.
children.committeeDescription: Campaigns & Copywriting develops the voice of TEDxGUC — from taglines and social captions to email newsletters and press releases.
children.memberBio: Malak is a talented copywriter and campaign strategist who brings the TEDxGUC message to life through compelling storytelling. Her work balances creative expression with strategic thinking to maximize impact and engagement.

events.recap: Our 2025 Spring event brought together 8 speakers across technology, health, design, and culture to explore how we can redefine the future. With over 1000 attendees, the event featured immersive experiences, networking sessions, and thought-provoking talks that challenged conventional thinking.
TEDxGUC 'Uncharted' explored the unknown territories of human potential. From design thinking to mental health advocacy, our speakers pushed audiences to venture beyond their comfort zones.

speakers.name: Karim Nabil
speakers.talkTitle: Breaking the Silence on Mental Health

events.recap: Our 'Mosaic' event celebrated the beauty of diverse perspectives coming together. Speakers shared stories of entrepreneurship, storytelling, and cultural preservation.

upcomingEvent.description: Ideas that ignite change. TEDxGUC 'upsidedown' will bring together innovators, artists, and thinkers who spark transformation in their communities.

upcomingSchedule.title: Doors Open & Registration
upcomingSchedule.description: Check-in, grab your badge, and enjoy coffee.

upcomingSchedule.title: Session 1 — Spark
upcomingSchedule.description: Three talks exploring the origins of transformative ideas.

upcomingSchedule.title: Networking Break
upcomingSchedule.description: Connect with fellow attendees over refreshments.

upcomingSchedule.title: Session 2 — Ignite
upcomingSchedule.description: Three talks on turning ideas into action.

upcomingSchedule.title: Lunch Break
upcomingSchedule.description: Catered lunch and interactive experience booths.

upcomingSchedule.title: Session 3 — Transform
upcomingSchedule.description: Three closing talks on lasting impact and legacy.

upcomingSchedule.title: Closing & Networking
upcomingSchedule.description: Closing remarks followed by open networking.

upcomingFAQs.question: What is the dress code?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Smart casual is recommended. Please dress comfortably as the event runs for a full day. Avoid overly casual attire like shorts or flip-flops.

upcomingFAQs.question: Are refunds available?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Tickets are non-refundable but transferable. If you can't attend, you may transfer your ticket to someone else by contacting us at least 48 hours before the event.

upcomingFAQs.question: What is the filming policy?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Professional filming will take place throughout the event. By attending, you consent to being photographed or filmed. Personal recording of talks is not permitted.

upcomingFAQs.question: Is parking available?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Free parking is available at the GUC campus. We recommend arriving early as spaces fill up quickly.

upcomingFAQs.question: Can I bring a guest?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Each ticket admits one person. If you'd like to bring a guest, they will need their own ticket.
